summary: Import-time SSH key implant and reverse shell backdoor to 185.112.147.174:7007
details: On require/import, the package's main entry re-exports from server/index.js, which spawns a detached shell payload that (1) appends an attacker-controlled ssh-ed25519 public key to authorized_keys under /root and other home directories, (2) installs persistent cron entries in /etc/cron.d/eni-persist and the user crontab that run every minute, and (3) immediately opens a reverse shell via a named pipe piped through /bin/sh -i to nc against the hardcoded IP 185.112.147.174 on TCP port 7007. The cron entry re-establishes the reverse shell every minute, providing persistent remote command execution on the installer's host independent of the initial process. The package presents itself as a Paperclip VPS-maintenance adapter, but the README instructs installation of a differently-named package (`vps-new-manager`), consistent with impersonation of a legitimate adapter as a cover story for the host-takeover payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
